basic_details.job_title: Indian Army NCC Special Entry Scheme 125th Course (Men) Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 70 Posts
basic_details.post_name: Short Service Commission (NT) for Men, including Wards of Battle Casualties
basic_details.short_information: Candidates searching for a quick snapshot of the NCC Special Entry Scheme 125th Course Recruitment 2026 can refer to the table below. It summarises the organisation, post, total vacancies, application window, and mode of application in one place.

age_limit.details: Applicants must be between 19 and 25 years as on 01 January 2027 , meaning candidates born not earlier than 02 January 2002 and not later than 01 January 2008 (both dates inclusive) are eligible. No age relaxation details beyond this are specified in the notification.

qualification.details: Candidates wondering about eligibility for the NCC Special Entry Scheme 125th Course can note that the entry is open only to unmarried male graduates who meet specific age, nationality, and NCC certification requirements. Wards of Battle Casualties have a slightly relaxed set of conditions, explained separately below. | A candidate must be a citizen of India, a subject of Nepal, or a person of Indian origin who has migrated from Pakistan, Myanmar, Sri Lanka, or specified East African countries with the intention of permanently settling in India, subject to a certificate of eligibility from the Government of India (not required for Gorkha subjects of Nepal). | Applicants must be between 19 and 25 years as on 01 January 2027, meaning candidates born not earlier than 02 January 2002 and not later than 01 January 2008 (both dates inclusive) are eligible. No age relaxation details beyond this are specified in the notification. | For NCC 'C' Certificate holders, the minimum qualification is a Bachelor's Degree from a recognised university, along with a minimum of 'B' Grade in the 'C' Certificate examination of NCC, after serving at least two/three years (as applicable) in the Senior Division/Wing of NCC. Final-year students may also apply, provided they complete their degree before the course begins; for such candidates, the aggregate percentage scored in the first two/three years of the degree course will be used for shortlisting. A provisional NCC 'C' Certificate will not be accepted at the SSB stage - candidates must hold the confirmed certificate before appearing for the interview. | Unmarried sons and unmarried daughters (including legally adopted) of Army personnel classified as Battle Casualties - killed in action, died of wounds, wounded/injured, or missing presumed dead - are eligible under a separate quota of 7 vacancies. The same graduation qualification applies, but an NCC 'C' Certificate is not required for this category.

salary_details.details: Selected candidates commissioned as Lieutenant draw pay as per Level 10 (₹56,100 – ₹1,77,500) in the Defence Pay Matrix, along with Military Service Pay of ₹15,500 per month. The approximate CTC on commissioning is ₹17–18 lakh per annum, excluding free medical cover and one home-town travel benefit per year. | During the 49-week training period at OTA Chennai, cadets receive a fixed stipend of ₹56,100 per month. On successful commissioning, this training period is not counted as commissioned service, though admissible allowance arrears for the training period are paid to cadets.

selection_process: The selection procedure for the NCC Special Entry Scheme 125 Course follows a five-stage structure: Application
followed by the Joining Letter. Each stage is explained below with the realistic timeline indicated in the notification.
Shortlisting of Applications: Based on bonus marks out of a maximum of 100 - up to 50 marks for performance in the NCC 'C' Certificate (50 for 'A' Grade

25 for 'B' Grade) and up to 50 marks for graduation performance (ranging from 0 for below 60% to 50 for 85% and above). Shortlisting is tentatively expected in the first week of September 2026. SSB Centre & Date Selection: Shortlisted candidates choose their SSB centre and date on a first-come-first-served basis during a 14-day window in September 2026. SSB Interview: Conducted over a maximum of five days at Prayagraj

likely in November/December 2026. It follows a two-stage process - candidates who fail Stage I are returned the same day. Medical Examination: Candidates recommended after Stage II undergo a medical test; the medical validity period is 270 days from the SSB medical board date to the PCTA joining date. Merit List: Prepared strictly on SSB interview marks; ties are broken first by age (older candidate ranked higher) and then by qualifying exam percentage. Joining Letter: Issued to candidates within the merit list and available vacancies
subject to meeting all eligibility criteria
for Pre-Commission Training at OTA Chennai starting April 2027.

content_sections.heading: Indian Army NCC Special Entry Scheme 125 Course PDF Download Link
content_sections.text: Candidates who want to verify every clause before applying should download and read the complete NCC Special Entry Scheme 125th Course notification PDF directly from the official Indian Army recruiting website. The PDF carries full details on medical standards, physical fitness benchmarks, document checklists, and terms of engagement that are only summarised in this article. content_sections.heading: Indian Army Officer Recruitment 2026 Important Dates 2026
content_sections.text: Anyone tracking the NCC 125 Course last date and other key milestones should note the schedule below. These dates are as specified in the official notification and cover the application window through the likely SSB period.

rows.event: Shortlisting for SSB (Tentative, based on Bonus Marks)

content_sections.heading: Indian Army NCC Special Entry Scheme 125 Course Vacancy 2026
content_sections.text: The NCC Special Entry Scheme 125 Course vacancy 2026 stands at a total of 70 posts under the Short Service Commission (Non-Tech) category. These vacancies are split between general NCC candidates and a reserved category for wards of Army battle casualties. Candidates should also note that the Indian Army notifies vacancies separately for each entry and gender, and the final selection is purely based on the notified vacancies against the applications received for this specific course.

rows.category: Wards of Battle Casualties of Indian Army Personnel

content_sections.heading: Indian Army NCC Special Entry Scheme 125 Course Eligibility 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates wondering about eligibility for the NCC Special Entry Scheme 125th Course can note that the entry is open only to unmarried male graduates who meet specific age, nationality, and NCC certification requirements. Wards of Battle Casualties have a slightly relaxed set of conditions, explained separately below. A candidate must be a citizen of India, a subject of Nepal, or a person of Indian origin who has migrated from Pakistan, Myanmar, Sri Lanka, or specified East African countries with the intention of permanently settling in India, subject to a certificate of eligibility from the Government of India (not required for Gorkha subjects of Nepal). Applicants must be between 19 and 25 years as on 01 January 2027 , meaning candidates born not earlier than 02 January 2002 and not later than 01 January 2008 (both dates inclusive) are eligible. No age relaxation details beyond this are specified in the notification. For NCC 'C' Certificate holders, the minimum qualification is a Bachelor's Degree from a recognised university, along with a minimum of 'B' Grade in the 'C' Certificate examination of NCC , after serving at least two/three years (as applicable) in the Senior Division/Wing of NCC. Final-year students may also apply, provided they complete their degree before the course begins; for such candidates, the aggregate percentage scored in the first two/three years of the degree course will be used for shortlisting. A provisional NCC 'C' Certificate will not be accepted at the SSB stage - candidates must hold the confirmed certificate before appearing for the interview. Unmarried sons and unmarried daughters (including legally adopted) of Army personnel classified as Battle Casualties - killed in action, died of wounds, wounded/injured, or missing presumed dead - are eligible under a separate quota of 7 vacancies. The same graduation qualification applies, but an NCC 'C' Certificate is not required for this category.

content_sections.heading: Indian Army NCC Special Entry Scheme 125 Course Selection Process 2026
content_sections.text: The selection procedure for the NCC Special Entry Scheme 125 Course follows a five-stage structure: Application, Shortlisting, SSB Interview, Medical Examination, and Merit List, followed by the Joining Letter. Each stage is explained below with the realistic timeline indicated in the notification. Shortlisting of Applications: Based on bonus marks out of a maximum of 100 - up to 50 marks for performance in the NCC 'C' Certificate (50 for 'A' Grade, 25 for 'B' Grade) and up to 50 marks for graduation performance (ranging from 0 for below 60% to 50 for 85% and above). Shortlisting is tentatively expected in the first week of September 2026. SSB Centre & Date Selection: Shortlisted candidates choose their SSB centre and date on a first-come-first-served basis during a 14-day window in September 2026. SSB Interview: Conducted over a maximum of five days at Prayagraj, Bhopal, Bengaluru, or Jalandhar, likely in November/December 2026. It follows a two-stage process - candidates who fail Stage I are returned the same day. Medical Examination: Candidates recommended after Stage II undergo a medical test; the medical validity period is 270 days from the SSB medical board date to the PCTA joining date. Merit List: Prepared strictly on SSB interview marks; ties are broken first by age (older candidate ranked higher) and then by qualifying exam percentage. Joining Letter: Issued to candidates within the merit list and available vacancies, subject to meeting all eligibility criteria, for Pre-Commission Training at OTA Chennai starting April 2027.

content_sections.heading: Indian Army NCC Special Entry Scheme 125 Course Apply Online 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates looking to complete the NCC Special Entry Scheme 125 Course apply online process can follow the numbered steps below. Applications are accepted only through the official Indian Army recruiting website - no offline or hard-copy submissions are entertained. Visit www.joinindianarmy.nic.in and click on 'Officer Entry Application/Login'. Click 'Registration' and complete the registration form (skip this step if already registered on the portal). After registering, click 'Apply Online' under the Dashboard, then select 'Eligibility' on the Officers Selection page. Click 'Apply' against the "Short Service Commission NCC Special Entry Course" option. Fill in personal information, communication details, education details, and previous SSB details across each segment, clicking 'Save & Continue' after every section. Review all details on the 'Summary of your information' page and click 'Submit' only after confirming accuracy. Take two printouts of the submitted application (with Roll Number) 30 minutes after final closure of the online application on the last day. Candidates must submit only one application; multiple applications from the same candidate will result in cancellation of candidature, with no representation entertained.

content_sections.heading: Salary and Pay Scale After Commissioning
content_sections.text: Selected candidates commissioned as Lieutenant draw pay as per Level 10 (₹56,100 – ₹1,77,500) in the Defence Pay Matrix, along with Military Service Pay of ₹15,500 per month. The approximate CTC on commissioning is ₹17–18 lakh per annum , excluding free medical cover and one home-town travel benefit per year. During the 49-week training period at OTA Chennai, cadets receive a fixed stipend of ₹56,100 per month . On successful commissioning, this training period is not counted as commissioned service, though admissible allowance arrears for the training period are paid to cadets.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required at the Selection Centre
content_sections.text: Candidates must carry originals along with self-attested copies of the following documents to the Selection Centre; DigiLocker certificates are not accepted in place of originals unless the issuing institution certifies that only e-certificates were issued. Signed printout of the application with a self-attested photograph Matriculation/Secondary School Examination Certificate and Mark Sheet 12th Class Certificate and Mark Sheet Graduation Degree/Provisional Degree from a recognised university (provisional degree not older than six months as on 01 April 2027) Mark sheets of all years/semesters NCC 'C' Certificate, issued on or before the date of online application submission (not required for Wards of Battle Casualties) University certificate on CGPA-to-marks conversion criteria, where applicable For final-year candidates, a certificate from the Principal/Head of Institution confirming the result will be declared by 01 April 2027 For Wards of Battle Casualties, additional documents including the Battle Casualty certificate and relationship certificate issued by the Manpower (Policy & Planning) Directorate

content_sections.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid While Applying
content_sections.text: Many applicants lose out on the NCC Special Entry Scheme 125 Course not due to ineligibility but due to avoidable errors during the application process. Keeping the following points in mind can help candidates avoid rejection. Do not submit the application with a provisional NCC 'C' Certificate if it hasn't been upgraded to the final certificate before the SSB - it will not be accepted at that stage. Double-check that your name, parents' names, and date of birth in the online application exactly match your Matriculation/Secondary School certificate, since any mismatch leads to cancellation. Convert your CGPA to percentage using your university's official formula before filling in the "Marks obtained in Graduation" field, as discrepancies found later can cancel your candidature. Once the application window closes, no corrections are entertained, so review the "Summary of your information" page carefully before hitting Submit. Avoid submitting more than one application - the system treats multiple applications from the same candidate as grounds for cancellation, not deduplication. Keep a buffer before the deadline; queries received less than three days before the closing date are not guaranteed a response.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the NCC Special Entry Scheme 125th Course 2026?
faq.answer: The online application closes on 20 August 2026 at 1500 hrs. Applications opened on 20 July 2026 at 1500 hrs.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in the NCC Special Entry Scheme 125 Course?
faq.answer: There are 70 total vacancies - 63 for the General category and 7 reserved for Wards of Battle Casualties of Indian Army personnel.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for NCC Special Entry Scheme 125th Course?
faq.answer: Candidates must be 19 to 25 years old as on 01 January 2027, i.e., born between 02 January 2002 and 01 January 2008 (both dates inclusive).

faq.question: Q4. What educational qualification is required for NCC Special Entry Scheme 125 Course eligibility?
faq.answer: A Bachelor's Degree from a recognised university along with a minimum 'B' Grade in the NCC 'C' Certificate examination. Final-year students can also apply if they complete their degree before the course starts.

faq.question: Q5. Is there an application fee for NCC Special Entry Scheme 125 Course?
faq.answer: The official notification does not specify any application fee for this entry.

faq.question: Q6. What is the salary during training and after commissioning?
faq.answer: Cadets receive a stipend of ₹56,100 per month during the 49-week training at OTA Chennai. After commissioning, the approximate CTC is ₹17–18 lakh per annum as a Lieutenant (Pay Level 10), excluding free medical cover and annual home-town travel.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for NCC Special Entry Scheme 125 Course?
faq.answer: The process includes Application, Shortlisting (based on bonus marks for NCC 'C' Certificate and graduation performance), SSB Interview, Medical Examination, Merit List, and finally the Joining Letter for training at OTA Chennai.

faq.question: Q8. Where will the SSB interview be conducted?
faq.answer: SSB interviews will be conducted at Selection Centres in Prayagraj, Bhopal, Bengaluru, and Jalandhar, likely in November/December 2026, for a maximum duration of five days.

faq.question: Q9. Can married candidates apply for this NCC Special Entry Scheme?
faq.answer: No, only unmarried male candidates (including unmarried Wards of Battle Casualties) are eligible to apply for this entry.

faq.question: Q10. What is the official website to apply online for NCC Special Entry Scheme 125 Course Bharti 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must apply only through www.joinindianarmy.nic.in. No other website or offline mode is valid for this recruitment.
